# SMTA — De-Supabase Core & Fix the Better-Auth Adapter

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Status:** Design — approved, ready for implementation planning
**Source findings:** `docs/superpowers/2026-06-23-smta-better-auth-integration-findings.md`
**Audited artifact:** `docs/superpowers/SMTA-better-auth-1782169582820.sql` (generated by `deploy.js --adapter better-auth`)

## Problem

The SQL emitted by the better-auth adapter does **not** load on plain PostgreSQL — it
aborts on the first `GRANT … TO authenticated`, because the role does not exist. The
findings doc was verified line-by-line against the codebase and is **accurate**.

### Confirmed root cause

`packages/cli/deploy.js` concatenates `@smta/core` scripts and then appends the adapter's
scripts (`[...corePaths, ...adapterPaths]`). `@smta/core` is still **Supabase-shaped**, so its
DDL runs first and fails on plain Postgres before any better-auth override is reached. The
better-auth adapter only overrides `core.get_current_user_id()` and the signup trigger; it
leaves the rest of core's Supabase coupling intact.

The architecture *intended* core to be adapter-agnostic — `packages/supabase/sql/constraints.sql`
literally says *"Restore auth.users FKs that core removed for adapter-agnosticism."* The
de-supabasing was **started but never finished**. Core still contains, hardcoded:

- **18× `auth.uid()`** calls (should be `core.get_current_user_id()` — the interface that already exists)
- **`REFERENCES auth.users(id)`** in `platform.platform_users` and `platform.tenant_secrets`
- the **`auth.users` signup trigger** in `core/sql/triggers/new_user.sql`
- **grants** to `authenticated` / `anon` / `service_role` (33×) and `app`-schema default privileges
- a Supabase **`supabase_user_id`** column + `auth.users` existence checks in `platform.*`

### Why 506 tests passed anyway

The pgTap harness (`run_tests.sh`, `TESTING.md`) runs against a **Supabase** database
(`supabase db reset`, port 54322), where `authenticated`/`anon`/`service_role` and `auth.users`
already exist. The harness silently supplies everything missing on plain Postgres, so it never
exercised — and never caught — any of these load failures. **The same defect affects the Payload
adapter** (shared core; ships no roles and no secrets impl), it simply hasn't been reported yet.

## Goals

1. `@smta/core` loads cleanly and standalone on vanilla PostgreSQL 18 (no `auth.*`, no Supabase roles).
2. `deploy.js --adapter better-auth` output loads on plain Postgres in a single transaction.
3. The Supabase adapter continues to work unchanged in behavior (no regression).
4. Payload is fixed as a side effect of an agnostic core.
5. A plain-Postgres CI gate prevents this class of regression from recurring.

## Non-goals

- No templating engine or build-time substitution (SQL stays plain and reviewable).
- No change to the row-level security *model* — RLS continues to key off `core.get_current_user_id()`,
  not Postgres role identity (policies apply to PUBLIC; 0 are role-targeted).
- No change to UUID typing in core. The Better-Auth id-format mismatch is solved in the adapter.

## Chosen approach

**Approach 1 — Self-contained agnostic core + adapter add-backs.** Make `@smta/core` valid on
its own; each adapter appends only its deltas. `deploy.js` keeps its existing dumb concatenation
(`core` then `adapter`) — no ordering logic needed, because core is valid standalone. (Rejected:
build-time templating, and per-adapter ordered post-patching — both add CLI machinery to avoid
editing core, but editing core *is* the fix.)

---

## Section 1 — Core role model

Core creates two neutral roles it owns, idempotently, at the top of `init/schemas.sql` (before any `GRANT`):

```sql
DO $$ BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_roles WHERE rolname = 'app_user')  THEN CREATE ROLE app_user  NOLOGIN; END IF;
  IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_roles WHERE rolname = 'app_admin') THEN CREATE ROLE app_admin NOLOGIN BYPASSRLS; END IF;
END $$;
```

- **`app_user`** — `NOLOGIN`, **RLS-subject** (no `BYPASSRLS`). The runtime identity the application
  backend assumes. Replaces every core grant currently aimed at `authenticated`. RLS still keys off
  `core.get_current_user_id()` (the session variable), not this role.
- **`app_admin`** — `NOLOGIN`, `BYPASSRLS`. Replaces `service_role` for migrations/seed/admin DML.
- **`anon`** is removed from core. The two public-by-design functions
  (`public.get_invitation_details`, `public.list_subscription_products`) keep their default `PUBLIC`
  `EXECUTE` grant; admin-function `REVOKE … FROM anon` lines become `REVOKE … FROM PUBLIC`.

**Critical correctness constraint:** the runtime backend MUST connect as a role that does **not**
bypass RLS (`app_user`), or session-variable enforcement is silently void. Migrations connect as
`app_admin` or the DB owner. Adapter docs state this explicitly.

## Section 2 — De-supabasing core DDL

Surgical, mechanical edits so core loads on vanilla Postgres:

| Finding | Change in `@smta/core` |
|---|---|
| A3, B5/B6 | Replace all **18 `auth.uid()`** → `core.get_current_user_id()`. |
| A1, B9 | `authenticated` → `app_user`, `service_role` → `app_admin` across `init/schemas.sql`, `tables/grants.sql`, `public/grants.sql`, `platform/*`. Remove `anon` refs per Section 1. |
| A2 | Remove `REFERENCES auth.users(id)` from `platform.platform_users` and `platform.tenant_secrets`. These FKs move to the **supabase adapter** (joining the ones already in its `constraints.sql`). |
| A4 | Remove the `auth.users` trigger from `core/sql/triggers/new_user.sql`. Signup wiring becomes an adapter concern entirely. |
| B5/B6 | Rename `platform.platform_users.supabase_user_id` → `user_id`; replace `auth.users` existence checks in `platform/functions/users.sql` and `public/functions/organizations.sql` with `core.users_meta` lookups. Update `platform.platform_action_logs.auth_user_id` FK and any `supabase_user_id` references in `platform/functions/*` and `platform/rls/lockdown.sql`. |
| C10 | Remove `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA app … TO <role>`. SMTA stops auto-granting on the shared `app` schema; the consuming project owns `app` privileges. |
| C11 | `graphql/disable_extension.sql` is moved out of core's manifest into the **supabase** manifest only (see Section 3). |

After these edits, `@smta/core` alone loads cleanly on plain Postgres.

## Section 3 — Adapter responsibilities

Each adapter package appends only its deltas; `deploy.js` concatenation order is unchanged.

- **`@smta/supabase`** — restore Supabase semantics so nothing regresses:
  - `GRANT app_user TO authenticated;` and `GRANT app_admin TO service_role;` (Supabase's roles
    inherit core's privileges). Decide `anon`'s grants explicitly (USAGE only where previously held).
  - Keep `auth.uid()`-backed `get_current_user_id()` and the Vault secrets impl.
  - `constraints.sql` gains the two `auth.users` FKs removed from core (`platform.platform_users`,
    `platform.tenant_secrets`) plus a signup trigger on `auth.users`.
  - `graphql/disable_extension.sql` enters the **supabase** manifest (still gated by `--enable-graphql`).
- **`@smta/payload`** — map the app connection to `app_user`/`app_admin`; add the pgcrypto secrets
  impl (Section 5). Inherits the now-agnostic core (fixes Payload's latent identical breakage).
- **`@smta/better-auth`** — role mapping + identity strategy (Section 4) + pgcrypto secrets (Section 5)
  + signup trigger. Non-Supabase adapters never reference `pg_graphql` at all.

## Section 4 — Better-Auth identity strategy (install-time flag)

The Better-Auth `user.id` is a random string (cuid) by default; core's user columns are `UUID`.
This is resolved by an **explicit, required install-time flag** on `deploy.js`, e.g.
`--better-auth-ids <uuid|mapped>`. The flag selects which SQL the adapter emits; **SMTA never
detects or branches at runtime.** If the flag is omitted for `--adapter better-auth`, `deploy.js`
errors with a message describing both modes (recommending `uuid` for performance, `mapped` for
zero Better-Auth config).

### `uuid` mode (the "ideal" path)

Prerequisite: the consumer sets Better-Auth `advanced.database.generateId` to emit UUIDs. Emits:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION core.get_current_user_id()
RETURNS UUID AS $$
  SELECT NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_user_id', true), '')::UUID;
$$ LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = core, public;
```

- Signup trigger inserts `NEW.id::UUID` directly into `core.users_meta`.
- No mapping table. Zero lookup overhead — identical to the Payload adapter.

### `mapped` mode (the "any id format" path)

No Better-Auth config required. Emits a mapping table and a lookup-based resolver:

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS core.user_identities (
  external_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                 -- Better-Auth user.id (cuid/string/uuid)
  user_id     UUID NOT NULL UNIQUE DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
);

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION core.get_current_user_id()
RETURNS UUID AS $$
  SELECT user_id
  FROM core.user_identities
  WHERE external_id = NULLIF(current_setting('app.current_user_id', true), '');
$$ LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = core, public;
```

- Signup trigger mints the UUID and writes both `core.user_identities` and `core.users_meta`
  (with `ON CONFLICT … DO NOTHING`), inside the existing `DO` block that attaches only if the
  Better-Auth `"user"` table exists.
- Performance: `external_id` is the PK (O(1)); the function is `STABLE` (result cached within a
  statement). Adequate for realistic workloads; optimize per-transaction later only if a hot path proves otherwise.

### Middleware (unchanged in both modes)

The session-variable pattern is fundamental and already present: the existing `withSMTA()` wrapper /
`inject-user-context.ts` sets `app.current_user_id` to the Better-Auth session id per
request/transaction. It stays thin — no translation, no in-memory cache. All id-format handling
lives in the emitted SQL. (Supabase's equivalent is PostgREST injecting JWT claims; Payload's is
the same wrapper pattern.)

## Section 5 — pgcrypto secrets implementation

`core.store_secret_impl` returns a value cast to `UUID` and stored in `tenant_secrets.vault_key_id`
(UUID column), mirroring the Vault impl. The pgcrypto impl uses a UUID-keyed encrypted store,
shared by `@smta/better-auth` and `@smta/payload`:

```sql
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS core.encrypted_secrets (
  id         UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name       TEXT,
  ciphertext BYTEA NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);
```

- `core.store_secret_impl(p_secret, p_name)` → `pgp_sym_encrypt(p_secret, <key>)`, insert, return `id::TEXT`.
- `core.delete_secret_impl(p_secret_ref)` → `DELETE … WHERE id = p_secret_ref::UUID`.
- `core.read_secret_impl(p_secret_ref)` → companion for retrieval (Vault read is implicit; make it
  explicit so secret-consuming flows such as SMTP work). Note: this adds a function to the secrets
  interface; the Supabase adapter gains a matching Vault-backed `read_secret_impl`.
- **Encryption key** comes from a GUC (e.g. `current_setting('app.secrets_key')`) set by the backend
  per session/connection — never hard-coded in SQL. Missing key raises a clear error.

## Section 6 — Verification (plain-Postgres CI)

The gap that masked everything was that the harness only ran on Supabase. Close it:

- **Load test (primary gate):** a CI job spins up stock `postgres:18`, runs `deploy.js` for
  `--adapter better-auth --better-auth-ids uuid`, `--adapter better-auth --better-auth-ids mapped`,
  and `--adapter payload`, applies each output in a single `psql -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 --single-transaction`,
  and asserts a clean load with **zero** undefined-role / `auth.*` errors. A `--adapter supabase`
  load runs against an image/shim that provides the Supabase prerequisites (`auth`, `vault`, role
  trio) to prove the add-backs still work. Automates the findings-doc dry-run.
- **Functional tests on plain Postgres:** parameterize `run_tests.sh` so the existing pgTap suite can
  run against vanilla Postgres using `app_user`/`app_admin` and a Better-Auth `"user"` table fixture
  (replacing the Supabase `auth.users` fixture). Proves RLS actually enforces under the
  single-app-role + session-variable model.
- **`tests/adapters/`** gains a Payload load/smoke test alongside the better-auth one, and a
  `mapped`-mode user-sync test (signup → `core.user_identities` + `core.users_meta` rows; RLS resolves).

## Findings → resolution map

| Finding | Resolved by |
|---|---|
| A1 — PostgREST roles never created | §1 neutral roles; §3 supabase re-grants |
| A2 — `REFERENCES auth.users` in DDL | §2 (remove from core); §3 supabase `constraints.sql` |
| A3 — `auth.uid()` in SQL bodies | §2 (→ `core.get_current_user_id()`) |
| A4 — `CREATE TRIGGER ON auth.users` | §2 (remove from core); §3 supabase trigger |
| B5 — `platform.*` Supabase-coupled | §2 (rename `supabase_user_id`, swap lookups) |
| B6 — `create_platform_user(p_supabase_user_id)` | §2 (rename param, validate vs `core.users_meta`) |
| B7 — secrets stubs unimplemented | §5 pgcrypto impl |
| B8 — Better-Auth string id vs UUID | §4 install-time flag (`uuid`/`mapped`) |
| B9 — dead `anon`/`service_role` grants | §1 (drop/remap); §3 supabase maps trio |
| C10 — default privileges on `app` schema | §2 (remove; consuming project owns `app`) |
| C11 — `DROP EXTENSION pg_graphql` | §2/§3 (supabase manifest only) |

## Affected files (indicative, not exhaustive)

- **core:** `sql/init/schemas.sql`, `sql/init/auth_interface.sql`, `sql/init/secrets_interface.sql`,
  `sql/tables/grants.sql`, `sql/public/grants.sql`, `sql/triggers/new_user.sql`,
  `sql/platform/tables/users.sql`, `sql/platform/tables/tenant_secrets.sql`,
  `sql/platform/tables/action_logs.sql`, `sql/platform/functions/*.sql`, `sql/platform/rls/lockdown.sql`,
  `sql/public/functions/organizations.sql`, `sql/public/functions/secrets.sql`,
  `sql/tables/audit_logs.sql`, `sql-scripts.json` (drop `graphql/disable_extension.sql`).
- **supabase adapter:** `sql/constraints.sql` (+2 FKs, signup trigger), new grant-mapping file,
  `secrets_supabase_impl.sql` (+`read_secret_impl`), `sql-scripts.json` (add graphql disable + grants).
- **better-auth adapter:** new `uuid`/`mapped` variant SQL files, pgcrypto secrets impl, role-mapping
  file, `sql-scripts.json` restructured for flag-selected variants.
- **payload adapter:** role-mapping file, pgcrypto secrets impl, `sql-scripts.json`.
- **cli:** `deploy.js` (`--better-auth-ids` flag, required for better-auth; variant file selection).
- **tests/CI:** new plain-Postgres load job, parameterized `run_tests.sh`, new adapter tests.
- **docs:** adapter READMEs (role model, RLS-subject connection requirement, `--better-auth-ids` modes,
  "UUID is ideal" guidance, pgcrypto key configuration).
